Confirmation of the pattern of dominance of subject groups comes from the mention of cities and other evidences of a civilized way of life among the Lamanites. The brief Nephite record does not bother to tell how the transition from the early nomadic Lamanite pattern to settled life occurred, but the text assures us that change they did, at least some of them. By the time the sons of Mosiah…

Synagogues after the order of the Nehors] The Amalekites and the Amulonites had not eschewed all religion but rather had embraced one that justified their iniquities and fed their hatred and wrath for those who preached the doctrine of Christ. The Book of Mormon gives us an excellent chance to compare the skepticism, disbelief, and pride of our time with that which prevailed in circumstances centuries ago: Therefore, as Aaron entered into one of their synagogues to preach unto the people, and as he was speaking unto them, behold there arose an Amalekite and began to contend with him, saying: What is that thou hast testified? Hast thou…

"Therefore, as Aaron entered into one of their synagogues to preach unto the people, and as he was speaking unto them, behold there arose an Amalekite and began to contend with him, saying: What is that thou hast testified? Hast thou seen an angel? Why do not angels appear unto us? Behold are not this people as good as thy people? (Alma 21:5.) We also see rationalzation in the raw…Likewise open…

"Now, Aaron began to open the scriptures unto them concerning the coming of Christ, and also concerning the resurrection of the dead, and that there could be no redemption for mankind, save it were through the death and sufferings of Christ, and the atonement of His blood" (Page 303).

We traditionally tend to speak of four, which is the only number ever given of them outright (Mosiah 27:34, "And four of them were the sons of Mosiah; and their names were Ammon, and Aaron, and Omner, and Himni; these were the names of the sons of Mosiah"). However, we should bear in mind that these were the sons of Mosiah2 who (a) rebelled, and (b) were alive at the time of the youth of Alma2.…
